pi_items.verified_value: ["4920523"] (4904210 is incorrect - it is the STCC for ammonia, not chlorine)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed 4920523 is STCC for chlorine (Class 2.3, poison gas); 4904210 is STCC for anhydrous ammonia (Class 2.3, per STCC_HazMatFileLayout.pdf)
DOT Hazard Class 2.3 confirmed for chlorine (49 CFR §173.115); CAMEO Chemicals lists 'Poison Gas' label; classified as toxic gas under ERG Guide 124
STCC code 4905792 confirmed for vinyl chloride via multiple sources including chemical databases and HazMat shipping guides
DOT Hazard Class 2.1 confirmed for vinyl chloride, stabilized (UN1086); CAMEO Chemicals classifies as Flammable Gas
STCC code 4907265 confirmed for styrene/styrene monomer via chemical databases and transportation guides
DOT Hazard Class 3 confirmed for styrene monomer (UN2055); CAMEO Chemicals classifies as Flammable Liquid; 49 CFR §173.120 defines Class 3
STCC code 4905781 confirmed for propane via web search and shipping guides
DOT Hazard Class 2.1 confirmed for propane (UN1978); CAMEO Chemicals classifies as Flammable Gas
STCC code 4904210 confirmed as primary code for anhydrous ammonia; verified in multiple hazmat shipping resources and Anhydrous Ammonia safety data sheets
STCC code 4920359 confirmed as TDG (Transportation of Dangerous Goods) code for ammonia; identified in Anhydrous Ammonia safety data sheet
Unable to verify through web search; no results found for STCC code 4904877 in any transportation databases or resources
Verified through 49 CFR §172.101: UN1005 ammonia has two entries - domestic as 2.2 (non-flammable gas) and international as 2.3 (poison gas); CAMEO Chemicals confirms both

pi_items.verified_value: CAMEO Chemicals does not provide STCC codes on their datasheets
pi_items.source: All four CAMEO Chemicals pages (vinyl chloride, styrene, propane, ammonia) do not contain STCC fields; chlorine page also lacks STCC; STCC codes must be obtained from external databases (Railinc, Association of American Railroads)

privileged_info_summary: All five chemicals in the task are real: Chlorine (UN1017, Class 2.3), Vinyl Chloride stabilized (UN1086, Class 2.1), Styrene monomer stabilized (UN2055, Class 3), Propane (UN1978, Class 2.1), and Ammonia anhydrous (UN1005, Class 2.2/2.3). STCC codes verified: Chlorine 4920523, Vinyl Chloride 4905792, Styrene 4907265, Propane 4905781, Ammonia 4904210 and 4920359. However, CAMEO Chemicals website does not provide STCC codes—agents would need external resources. One critical issue: the verifier lists STCC 4904210 (ammonia) as an indicator for chlorine, which is incorrect; chlorine's STCC is 4920523.
